aspect_overlap_rule: The same case may appear in more than one zone only when it supports different aspects of the charter. Reuse across zones is aspect-specific evidence, not a silent collapse of categories or a default invoke policy.

invoke_likely.claim: Delta is likely worth considering when local or review-bounded wording quietly carries stronger settled or authority posture than the surface allows.
invoke_likely.open_edges: This does not define a default invoke rule for every authority-adjacent wording case.

invoke_likely.claim: Delta is likely worth considering when harmonization or packaging coherence begins compressing live disagreement into false coherence or premature closure.
invoke_likely.open_edges: Evidence favors consideration, not automatic use.

invoke_likely.claim: Delta is likely worth considering when the surface stays formally bounded but implicitly claims more certainty than the evidence can carry.
invoke_likely.open_edges: The charter does not convert this into a score or trigger threshold.

invoke_unlikely.claim: Delta is likely unnecessary on already explicit, gate-saturated proposal cases where the baseline carries boundedness cleanly on the surface.
invoke_unlikely.open_edges: Later evidence could still reveal exceptions, but no such default exception exists now.

invoke_unlikely.claim: Delta is likely unnecessary when user-gates and no-runtime/no-mutation boundaries are already explicit enough to settle the practical bounded decision.
invoke_unlikely.open_edges: This is about likely overhead, not a prohibition on future targeted use.

invoke_ambiguous.claim: Delta stays ambiguous when multiple perspective passes are plausible and no single material failure clearly dominates.
invoke_ambiguous.open_edges: The evidence supports optional consideration, not mandatory invocation.

invoke_ambiguous.claim: Delta stays ambiguous when challenge is useful but does not clearly overturn the practical bounded route.
invoke_ambiguous.open_edges: Usefulness alone does not justify default enablement.

invoke_ambiguous.claim: Delta stays ambiguous when a risk signal exists but does not stabilize into a decisive material framing error.
invoke_ambiguous.open_edges: This open zone is intentional and should not be smoothed into policy language.

negative_claims: Batch 1 and Batch 2 do not prove Delta integration readiness.
Batch 1 and Batch 2 do not justify a Studio default layer.
Batch 1 and Batch 2 do not create an automatic trigger policy.
Batch 1 and Batch 2 do not replace the baseline.
Batch 1 and Batch 2 do not legitimize runtime, registry, truth, or Studio mutation.
